docs.title: Rock Paper Scissors CI/CD Project
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for the Rock Paper Scissors university project. This project focuses on modern software engineering practices, including automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.

docs.title: Project Objectives
docs.text:
• Functional Mini Application: A robust RPS engine with "Best of N" logic.
  • CI/CD Pipeline: Fully automated testing and deployment via GitHub Actions.
  • Automated Testing: Comprehensive Whitebox and Blackbox testing suites.
  • Professional Documentation: Utilizing MkDocs for high-quality technical documentation.

docs.title: Artifact Under Study
docs.text: The core of this project is game.js, a JavaScript module that manages the game state, validates moves, and determines winners based on a configurable round count.

docs.title: Data Model & Schema
docs.text: This section documents the data structures and schemas used by the RPS engine.

docs.title: 1. Game (Entity: RPSGame)
docs.text:
Represents a single match consisting of multiple rounds.

Attribute Type Description bestOf Integer Total rounds to be played (Must be odd). winThreshold Integer Rounds required to win the game. p1Score Integer Current score for Player 1. p2Score Integer Current score for Player 2. gameOver Boolean Whether the match has concluded. winner String The winner of the match (p1, p2, or null).

docs.title: 2. Round (Entity: RoundResult)
docs.text:
Represents the outcome of a single encounter.

Attribute Type Description roundNumber Integer The sequence number of the round. p1Move String Move chosen by Player 1. p2Move String Move chosen by Player 2. result String Result of the round (p1, p2, or draw).

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• A Game consists of 1 to N Rounds.
  • Each Round is associated with exactly one Game.

docs.title: Game State
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "GameState",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "bestOf": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1 },
    "winThreshold": { "type": "integer" },
    "p1Score": { "type": "integer" },
    "p2Score": { "type": "integer" },
    "gameOver": { "type": "boolean" },
    "winner": { "type": ["string", "null"] },
    "roundsPlayed": { "type": "integer" }
  }
}

docs.title: Testing Strategy
docs.text: This project employs a dual-layered testing approach to ensure reliability and robustness.

docs.title: Whitebox Testing
docs.text: Whitebox testing focuses on the internal structure and logic of the code.

docs.title: 1. Statement & Block Coverage
docs.text: We ensure that every line of code in game.js is executed at least once during the test suite.

docs.title: 2. Path & Condition Coverage
docs.text: The determineRoundResult function contains multiple nested if/else blocks. Our tests cover every possible logical path (e.g., Rock vs Paper, Paper vs Scissors, etc.) to ensure no edge case is left unhandled.

docs.title: Blackbox Testing
docs.text: Blackbox testing focuses on the specifications and requirements without looking at the internal code.

docs.title: 1. Boundary Value Analysis (BVA)
docs.text: We test the bestOf parameter in the RPSGame constructor at its boundaries: - Minimum: 1 (Valid) - Below Minimum: 0 (Invalid) - Maximum: 99 (Valid) - Above Maximum: 101 (Invalid) - Even Number: 2 (Invalid - requires odd for no draws)

docs.title: 2. Equivalence Class Partitioning (ECP)
docs.text: We partition the inputs for move validation: - Valid Class: {'rock', 'paper', 'scissors'} (including variations like ' ROCK '). - Invalid Class: {'lizard', 123, null, undefined}.

docs.title: Automation
docs.text: Tests are automated using Jest and integrated into the CI/CD pipeline. - Framework: Jest - CI Tool: GitHub Actions - Coverage Tool: Istanbul (built-in to Jest)

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide explains how to set up the Rock Paper Scissors project and use its core features.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js: Version 18 or higher is recommended.
  • npm: Usually comes bundled with Node.js.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone <repository-url>
cd TOPRPS

  2. Install dependencies: 

    npm install

docs.title: Running the Game
docs.text: The game logic is encapsulated in game.js. You can integrate it into any JavaScript environment.

docs.title: Example Integration
docs.text:
const { RPSGame } = require('./game');

// Initialize a "Best of 3" game
const game = new RPSGame(3);

// Play a round
const result = game.playRound('rock', 'scissors');
console.log(result); 
// Output: { roundResult: 'p1', p1Score: 1, p2Score: 0, gameOver: false, winner: null }

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
To execute the automated testing suite and generate a coverage report:

npm test
